channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden may visit Pope Francis in final international trip of his presidency, AP sources say
item.description: President Joe Biden is considering a visit to the Vatican to meet with Pope Francis in early January for what would likely be the final international trip of his presidency.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Government shutdown at risk as Trump's demands put Speaker Johnson in a bind
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A government shutdown at risk, House Speaker Mike Johnson is fighting to figure out how to meet President-elect Donald Trump's sudden demands — and keep his own job — while federal offices are being told to prepare to shutter operations ahead of Friday's midnight deadline.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some House Republicans privately furious at Musk, Ramaswamy after government shutdown talks implode
item.description: Some House Republicans are privately fuming at the 11th-hour involvement of Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy in government funding talks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rand Paul suggests replacing Mike Johnson with Elon Musk as Speaker of the House
item.description: Sen. Rand Paul, R-Ky., has floated the idea of replacing House Speaker Mike Johnson, R-La., with Elon Musk after the chaotic collapse of the continuing resolution spending bill.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans from hurricane-ravaged states warn against stripping disaster aid from spending bill
item.description: Senate Republicans from regions affected by hurricanes and other storms are warning negotiators against stripping disaster funding from the stopgap spending bill that is suddenly in turmoil after President-elect Trump heavily criticized Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) plan.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers caught off guard by Trump debt ceiling demand
item.description: Lawmakers on both sides of the aisle appeared caught off guard on Wednesday as President-elect Trump demanded that a debt ceiling hike be paired with a stopgap government funding bill, rejecting a sweeping bipartisan plan to avert a government shutdown this week.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia appeals court disqualifies DA Fani Willis and her team from Trump election interference case
item.description: Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis and her office can no longer prosecute President-elect Trump following a Georgia court of appeals ruling.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A power grab by Republicans in North Carolina becomes a referendum on democracy in the states
item.description: Democrats in North Carolina were celebrating big wins in the swing state after the November election, including victories for governor and other top statewide offices.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando And Gary D. Robertson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tim Scott's Senate campaign arm staff revealed ahead of crucial 2026 swing state races
item.description: Sen. Tim Scott is bringing on a slate of GOP veterans with a variety of political experiences as he looks to expand the Republican Senate majority in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: After losing VP bid, Walz searches for role in pushing back against Trump
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz saw his national profile skyrocket when he became Vice President Harris’ running mate. Now, he’s back home figuring out when he will push back or work with Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: DOGE Caucus leader Joni Ernst eyes relocation out of DC for third of federal workers
item.description: Relocating federal workers and mandating a return to the office are targets for DOGE Caucus leader Joni Ernst ahead of Trump's second term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A key Trump ally on immigration explains how mass deportations could work
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — A Kansas official who is an informal adviser to President-elect Donald Trump's transition team on immigration issues doesn't expect mass deportations to prompt arrests of migrants at sensitive locations such as schools and churches.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jeffries suggests Democrats will oppose a 'clean' funding bill after Johnson's plan falters
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) suggested Wednesday that Democrats will oppose any federal spending bill that strays from the bipartisan deal announced a day prior, accusing GOP leaders of reneging on the agreement and risking a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Andy Beshear says Democrats should set partisanship aside, work to meet people's needs
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear said Wednesday that Democrats should be thinking less about partisan politics and more about meeting people's everyday needs as they chart a comeback strategy after last month's crushing election losses.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate passes $895 billion Defense bill despite transgender care provision fight
item.description: Senators voted 85 to 14 in favor of passing the National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA), well more than the 60 needed in the 100-member upper chamber.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: DHS rolls out new rules for skilled foreign workers on H-1B visas
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security on Tuesday announced new rules for hiring foreign workers that was met with praise for allowing employers to fill jobs quicker and maintaining strict security oversight
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: In last-ditch effort, Democrats push to get Julie Su confirmed as labor secretary
item.description: Acting Secretary Julie Su has led the Labor Department for nearly two years, despite never getting a Senate confirmation vote. With time running out, her staunchest supporters haven't given up.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Nevada's 6 Electoral College votes certified for President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Nevada Republican Party Chairman Michael McDonald officially certified the state's six Electoral College votes for President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday during a gathering on the UNR campus in Reno.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Horsford vows to defend Nevada families as priority for government cuts grows
item.description: Nevada Democratic Rep. Steven Horsford is doing more than just serving as a watchdog on Republican initiatives to cut government bureaucracy -- he's participating in the process.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minneapolis 2025 budget includes funding for immigrant-centered initiatives
item.description: The 2025 budget approved by the Minneapolis City Council last week designates resources for immigration legal services and other initiatives designed to help immigrant residents.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Trump seeks to smooth over concerns over RFK Jr
item.description: “I think he’s going to be much less radical than you would think,” Trump said during a wide-ranging news conference at Mar-a-Lago.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams denied public funds for 2025 reelection campaign: CFB
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams was denied public funds for his 2025 reelection campaign, the New York City Campaign Finance Board announced on Monday. The CFB cited the federal charges against Adams as one of the reasons why it denied him the money.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Lawmakers struggle to reach deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional negotiators are struggling to reach a deal to keep the government's lights on past Dec. 20. Text of a continuing resolution (CR) for a government funding package was expected Sunday ahead of the looming shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: RFK Jr.'s daughter-in-law met with Ratcliffe about CIA job: Washington Post
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s daughter-in-law took a private meeting with John Ratcliffe, President-elect Trump’s pick to run the CIA, in an apparent bid for a high-ranking position within the agency, The Washington Post reported.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, Fox 5
